words.definitionEn: except; apart from
words.exampleEn: We've had nothing but trouble with this car.

words.definitionEn: a small room on a ship in which you live or sleep
words.exampleEn: I lay in my cabin feeling miserably seasick.

words.definitionEn: a set of wires, covered in plastic or rubber, that carries electricity, phone signals, etc.
words.exampleEn: fibre-optic cable

words.definitionEn: to use numbers to find out a total number, amount, distance, etc.
words.exampleEn: calculate something, An independent valuer will calculate the value of your property.

words.definitionEn: a long straight passage dug in the ground and filled with water for boats and ships to travel along; a smaller passage used for carrying water to fields, crops, etc.
words.exampleEn: the Panama/Suez Canal

words.definitionEn: to decide that something that has been arranged will not now take place
words.exampleEn: All flights have been cancelled because of bad weather.

words.definitionEn: a serious disease in which growths of cells, also called cancers, form in the body and kill normal body cells. The disease often causes death.
words.exampleEn: breast/lung cancer

words.definitionEn: a round stick of wax with a piece of string (called a wick) through the middle that is lit to give light as it burns
words.exampleEn: a flickering candle

words.definitionEn: having the ability or qualities necessary for doing something
words.exampleEn: capable of something, You are capable of better work than this.

words.definitionEn: the number of things or people that a container or space can hold
words.exampleEn: a fuel tank with a maximum capacity of 50 litres

words.definitionEn: the act of capturing somebody/something or of being captured
words.exampleEn: He evaded capture for three days.

words.definitionEn: to catch a person or an animal and keep them as a prisoner or shut them in a space that they cannot escape from
words.exampleEn: Allied troops captured over 300 enemy soldiers.

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Carbon is found in all living things, existing in a pure state as diamond and graphite.
words.exampleEn: carbon fibre

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize something that is real or exists in fact
words.exampleEn: James looks younger than his wife but in actual fact (= really) he is five years older.

words.definitionEn: all the people who act in a play or film
words.exampleEn: an all-star cast (= including many well-known actors)

words.definitionEn: to look, smile, etc. in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: cast something + adv./prep., She cast a welcoming smile in his direction.

words.definitionEn: not formal
words.exampleEn: casual clothes (= comfortable clothes that you choose to wear in your free time)

words.definitionEn: an act of catching something, for example a ball
words.exampleEn: Roger made some brilliant catches at today's game.

words.definitionEn: a large hole in the side of a hill or cliff or under the ground
words.exampleEn: the mouth (= the entrance) of the cave

words.definitionEn: the smallest unit of living matter that can exist on its own. All plants and animals are made up of cells.
words.exampleEn: red and white blood cells

words.definitionEn: to change your behaviour in order to deal more successfully with a new situation
words.exampleEn: It's amazing how soon you adapt.

words.definitionEn: the state of being certain
words.exampleEn: There is no certainty that the president's removal would end the civil war.

words.definitionEn: an official document that may be used to prove that the facts it states are true
words.exampleEn: a birth/marriage/death certificate

words.definitionEn: to fasten something with a chain; to fasten somebody/something to another person or thing with a chain, so that they do not escape or get stolen
words.exampleEn: chain somebody/something, The doors were always locked and chained.

words.definitionEn: to act as the chairman or chairwoman of a meeting, discussion, etc.
words.exampleEn: to chair a committee

words.definitionEn: the person in charge of a meeting, who tells people when they can speak, etc.
words.exampleEn: Sir Herbert took it upon himself to act as chairman.

words.definitionEn: to question whether a statement or an action is right, legal, etc.; to refuse to accept something
words.exampleEn: challenge something, His legal team immediately sought to challenge the decision.

words.definitionEn: difficult in an interesting way that tests your ability
words.exampleEn: challenging work/questions/problems

words.definitionEn: a competition to find the best player or team in a particular sport
words.exampleEn: the National Basketball Association Championship

words.definitionEn: very typical of something or of somebody’s character
words.exampleEn: characteristic of something/somebody, Community support of families is characteristic of many societies.

words.definitionEn: a typical feature or quality that something/somebody has
words.exampleEn: There were few similarities in the brothers' physical characteristics.

words.definitionEn: very pleasant or attractive
words.exampleEn: The cottage is tiny, but it's charming.

words.definitionEn: to record or follow the progress or development of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: The exhibition charts the history of the palace.

words.definitionEn: the condition of being unable to stop using or doing something as a habit, especially something harmful
words.exampleEn: cocaine addiction

words.definitionEn: an act of running or driving after somebody/something in order to catch them or it
words.exampleEn: The thieves were caught by police after a short chase.

words.definitionEn: to run, drive, etc. after somebody/something in order to catch them or it
words.exampleEn: chase somebody/something, My dog likes chasing rabbits.

words.definitionEn: either side of the face below the eyes
words.exampleEn: chubby/rosy/pink cheeks

words.definitionEn: a shout of joy, support or praise
words.exampleEn: A great cheer went up from the crowd.

words.definitionEn: to shout loudly, in order to show support or praise for somebody, or to encourage them
words.exampleEn: We all cheered as the team came on to the field.

words.definitionEn: more than was first mentioned or is usual
words.exampleEn: additional resources/funds/security/funding/costs

words.definitionEn: most important
words.exampleEn: the chief cause/problem/reason

words.definitionEn: a person with a high rank or the highest rank in a company or an organization
words.exampleEn: army/police chiefs

words.definitionEn: a group of people who sing together, for example in church services or public performances
words.exampleEn: She sings in the school choir.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is more than was first mentioned or is usual
words.exampleEn: Additionally, the bus service will run on Sundays, every two hours.

words.definitionEn: to cut something into pieces with a sharp tool such as a knife
words.exampleEn: chop something, He was chopping logs for firewood.

words.definitionEn: a line, route or journey around a place
words.exampleEn: The race ended with eight laps of a city centre circuit.

words.definitionEn: the conditions and facts that are connected with and affect a situation, an event or an action
words.exampleEn: Police said there were no suspicious circumstances surrounding the boy's death.

words.definitionEn: to mention something as a reason or an example, or in order to support what you are saying
words.exampleEn: cite something, She cited examples of companies the city has helped relocate or expand.

words.definitionEn: a person who has the legal right to belong to a particular country
words.exampleEn: The defeat of the president did not change the lives of ordinary citizens for the better.

words.definitionEn: connected with the people who live in a country
words.exampleEn: civil unrest (= that is caused by groups of people within a country)

words.definitionEn: to think about a problem or a situation and decide how you are going to deal with it
words.exampleEn: address something, Your essay does not address the real issues.

words.definitionEn: a state of human society that is very developed and organized
words.exampleEn: the technology of modern civilization

words.definitionEn: to make something clearer or easier to understand
words.exampleEn: clarify something, to clarify a situation/problem/issue

words.definitionEn: accepted or deserving to be accepted as one of the best or most important of its kind
words.exampleEn: a classic film/story/car/game

words.definitionEn: a book, film or song which is well known and considered to be of very high quality, setting standards for other books, etc.
words.exampleEn: The film is now a cult classic.

words.definitionEn: enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need
words.exampleEn: They'll need an adequate supply of hot water.

words.definitionEn: to arrange something in groups according to features that they have in common
words.exampleEn: The books in the library are classified according to subject.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need
words.exampleEn: Are you adequately insured?

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to serve customers in a shop
words.exampleEn: The clerk at the counter gave me too little change.

words.definitionEn: a high area of rock with a very steep side, often at the edge of the sea or ocean
words.exampleEn: the cliff edge/top

words.definitionEn: a building or part of a hospital where people can go for special medical treatment or advice
words.exampleEn: Your local family planning clinic can give you advice about birth control.

words.definitionEn: a short part of a film that is shown separately
words.exampleEn: Here is a clip from her latest movie.

words.definitionEn: the end of a period of time or an activity
words.exampleEn: at the close of the seventeenth century

words.definitionEn: near in space or time
words.exampleEn: He walked into the room, closely followed by the rest of the family.

words.definitionEn: to change something slightly to make it more suitable for a new set of conditions or to make it work better
words.exampleEn: adjust something, Watch out for sharp bends and adjust your speed accordingly.

words.definitionEn: the fact of two things happening at the same time by chance, in a surprising way
words.exampleEn: a strange/an extraordinary/a remarkable coincidence

words.definitionEn: a sudden failure of something, such as an institution, a business or a course of action
words.exampleEn: the collapse of the Soviet Union

words.definitionEn: to fall down or fall in suddenly, often after breaking apart
words.exampleEn: The roof collapsed under the weight of snow.

words.definitionEn: a person who collects things, either as a hobby or as a job
words.exampleEn: a stamp collector

words.definitionEn: a country or an area that is governed by people from another, more powerful, country
words.exampleEn: former British colonies

words.definitionEn: full of bright colours or having a lot of different colours
words.exampleEn: colourful shop windows

words.definitionEn: the activities that are done in order to plan, organize and run a business, school or other institution
words.exampleEn: Administration costs are passed on to the customer.

words.definitionEn: two or more things joined or mixed together to form a single unit
words.exampleEn: combination of something, The tragedy was due to a combination of factors.

words.definitionEn: the state of being physically relaxed and free from pain; the state of having a pleasant life, with everything that you need
words.exampleEn: The hotel offers a high standard of comfort and service.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody who is worried or unhappy feel better by being kind and showing sympathy to them
words.exampleEn: comfort somebody, The victim's widow was today being comforted by family and friends.

words.definitionEn: humorous and making you laugh
words.exampleEn: a comic monologue/story

words.definitionEn: a magazine, usually for children, that tells stories through pictures
words.exampleEn: a comic book superhero

words.definitionEn: an order given to a person or an animal
words.exampleEn: Begin when I give the command.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: command somebody to do something, He commanded his men to retreat.

words.definitionEn: a person who is in charge of something, especially an officer in charge of a particular group of soldiers or a military operation
words.exampleEn: military/allied/field/flight commanders

words.definitionEn: an official group of people who have been given responsibility to control something, or to find out about something, usually for the government
words.exampleEn: The commission is expected to report its findings next month.

words.definitionEn: to officially ask somebody to write, make or create something or to do a task for you
words.exampleEn: commission somebody to do something, She has been commissioned to write a new national anthem.

words.definitionEn: a promise to do something or to behave in a particular way; a promise to support somebody/something; the fact of committing yourself
words.exampleEn: I'm not ready to make a long-term commitment.

words.definitionEn: a group of people who are chosen, usually by a larger group, to make decisions or to deal with a particular subject
words.exampleEn: an executive/advisory/organizing committee

words.definitionEn: usually; very often; by most people
words.exampleEn: Christopher is commonly known as Kit.

words.definitionEn: measured or judged by how similar or different it is to something else
words.exampleEn: Then he was living in comparative comfort (= compared with others or with his own life at a previous time).
